reasoning: In 2023, the total formal financial inclusion rate in Nigeria was tracked at just 74% by EFInA, with the fully banked portion at 64%. The actual target set by the CBN for 2024 in its NFIS 3.0 strategy was a 25% exclusion rate (i.e., 75% inclusion), nowhere near 95% banked. World Bank baseline in 2021 was 45% banked. It is structurally impossible for bank account ownership to jump from less than 64% to 95% within one year. Since the data definitively shows less than 95% of the population with a bank account through 2024, the criteria for negative resolution are met.
